claim: A business domain is represented as a directory at `domains/<name>/`.
why: Separates framework code (internal/, cmd/, the shared immutable framework body) from business content (domains/<name>/, per-business). Enables a future create_domain tool to scaffold new businesses without touching the framework body. Builds on the agent-as-directory pattern: just as agents are directories with their own CLAUDE.md+tools+agents (in the full layout), domains are directories with their own graph.json+manifest.json+docs/gen/. ENFORCED via check_domain_manifest_exists_and_importable, which is an HONEST NO-OP (it cannot reach the filesystem from the graph-only invariant contract); the structural guarantee that a domain is a discoverable directory is upheld by the loader (internal/loader/loader.go LoadGraph expects domains/<name>/graph.json) and the DOMAIN-MAP renderer (internal/generator/claudemd.go RenderDomainMapBlock walks domains/ on disk).
